sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: FinWise Bancorp (NASDAQ:FINW) Reports Mixed Q4 2025 Results with Revenue Beat and EPS Miss
feed.summary: FinWise Bancorp (NASDAQ:FINW) reported mixed Q4 2025 results, exceeding revenue expectations with $46.85 million but missing EPS estimates at $0.27 compared to $0.35. The company demonstrated strong growth, including record loan originations and a significant expansion in its credit-enhanced loan portfolio, leading to an expanded net interest margin. Despite a neutral market reaction, FinWise Bancorp highlighted its strategic shift towards a more capital-efficient lending model, with investors now watching for improved bottom-line performance.

feed.title: Form 424B2 BARCLAYS BANK PLC
feed.summary: Barclays Bank PLC has filed Form 424B2 for Barrier Digital Notes due March 4, 2027, linked to the performance of the Nasdaq-100, Russell 2000, and S&P 500 Indices. These notes do not pay interest and do not guarantee the full principal at maturity, offering a fixed return if the least performing underlier does not decline below its Digital Barrier Value. Investors are exposed to the market risk of each underlier, and any payment is subject to Barclays Bank PLC's creditworthiness and the risk of a U.K. Bail-in Power exercise.

feed.title: |【即時新聞】FGSI ETF公告月配息0.1435美元！預估年化殖利率達8.54%受矚目
feed.summary: |FT Vest Growth Strength & Target Income ETF (FGSI) announced a monthly dividend of $0.1435 per share, resulting in an estimated annualized yield of 8.54%. The ex-dividend date and record date were both January 21st, with the cash dividend expected to be paid on January 30th. This announcement highlights FGSI's competitive income potential for investors.

feed.title: News Corp Outlines US$1 Billion Buy-Back of Nasdaq-Listed Shares, Excludes ASX CDIs
feed.summary: News Corp has announced a US$1 billion share repurchase program for its Nasdaq-listed Class A and Class B common stock. This program explicitly excludes buy-backs of its ASX-listed CHESS Depositary Interests (CDIs), focusing capital returns on U.S.-listed stock. An analyst has rated AU:NWS stock as a Buy with a A$64.40 price target.

feed.title: Arthur J. Gallagher (AJG) Q4 Earnings: Taking a Look at Key Metrics Versus Estimates
feed.summary: Arthur J. Gallagher (AJG) reported strong Q4 2025 earnings, with revenue reaching $3.59 billion, a 34% year-over-year increase, and EPS at $2.38, up from $2.13 a year ago. The company exceeded Zacks Consensus Estimates for both revenue and EPS. While headline numbers were positive, some key operational metrics, such as compensation and operating expense ratios, were higher than analyst estimates in both the Brokerage and Risk Management segments.

feed.title: Kenvue Shareholder Meeting Shows Strong Support To Accept Kimberly-Clark’s Nearly $50Bn Offer
feed.summary: Kenvue's shareholder meeting indicated strong support for accepting Kimberly-Clark's offer of nearly $50 billion. The article, authored by Malcolm Spicer, highlights the evolving consumer health industry and features discussions with industry leaders. It covers topics such as OTC drugs, cosmetics, M&A deals, and business strategies.

feed.title: RBC Capital Sticks to Their Hold Rating for Oneok (OKE)
feed.summary: RBC Capital maintained its Hold rating on Oneok (OKE) with a price target of $79.00. Analyst Elvira Scotto, who covers the Energy sector, has a solid track record. This comes as Oneok reported strong quarterly revenues of $8.63 billion and a net profit of $939 million for the quarter ending September 30, and corporate insider sentiment is positive with recent share purchases.

feed.title: RBC Capital Sticks to Their Hold Rating for Oneok (OKE)
feed.summary: RBC Capital maintained a Hold rating on Oneok (OKE) with a $79.00 price target, following an analysis by Elvira Scotto. This comes amidst other analyst actions, with J.P. Morgan also issuing a Hold and Morgan Stanley maintaining a Buy rating. Oneok reported strong third-quarter earnings, with significant increases in revenue and net profit compared to the previous year, and positive insider sentiment shown by recent stock purchases.

feed.title: Thermo Fisher Scientific (TMO) Margin Improvement Challenges Long Term Earnings Decline Narrative
feed.summary: Thermo Fisher Scientific (TMO) concluded FY 2025 with strong fourth-quarter results, including US$12.2 billion in revenue and US$5.25 basic EPS, with net profit margins slightly up year-over-year at 15%. This recent performance challenges a prior five-year trend of earnings decline, with the latest year showing 5.8% earnings growth. While the P/E ratio is slightly below industry averages, suggesting a modest discount, concerns remain regarding debt coverage by operating cash flow.

feed.title: Akamai and Yospace Deliver Personalised Ads for Japan’s ABEMA
feed.summary: Akamai Technologies and Yospace have renewed their agreement with AbemaTV, Inc., to provide personalized ad experiences for ABEMA, a leading online video streaming service in Japan. This partnership leverages Yospace's server-side ad insertion (SSAI) technology, powered by Akamai's cloud platform, to deliver broadcast-quality, targeted ads seamlessly within live streams. The collaboration aims to enhance viewer experience by providing uninterrupted playback and advanced ad targeting while ensuring scalability and reliability for ABEMA's growing audience.

feed.title: CenterPoint Energy Stock: Quiet Climb, Defensive Power Play in a Jittery Market
feed.summary: CenterPoint Energy (CNP) stock has shown a steady upward trend amidst broader market volatility, signaling a shift towards cautious optimism among investors. With a solid dividend and improving earnings visibility, the utility is seen as a defensive play, reflected in its capital gains and total return over the past year. Analysts largely hold a "Buy" or "Overweight" rating, underscoring confidence in its capital investment program, regulatory navigation, and strategic positioning for grid modernization.

feed.title: The Truth About Costco Wholesale Corp.: Why Everyone Is Suddenly Obsessed With This Stock
feed.summary: This article examines the recent surge in popularity of Costco Wholesale Corp. stock, fueled by social media attention and the company's strong business model. It discusses why Costco is considered a premium stock, highlighting its membership-driven revenue, consistent value proposition, and robust market moat. The article advises potential investors to consider if they are looking for a long-term, steady-growth play rather than a quick flip, due to the stock's already high valuation.

feed.title: Actelis Networks Adjourns Special Meeting After Lacking Quorum
feed.summary: Actelis Networks (ASNS) adjourned its special shareholders' meeting on January 29, 2026, due to a lack of quorum, preventing a vote on issuing common stock beyond a 19.99% cap with White Lion Capital LLC. This ongoing issue hinders the company's ability to access further equity financing and necessitates another meeting within 90 days. The stock currently holds a "Hold" rating with a $0.46 price target, with TipRanks' AI Analyst Spark rating it "Neutral" due to weak financial performance and bearish technical momentum despite corporate event support.

feed.title: TKO Group officer Krauss sells $455k in class A common stock
feed.summary: Seth D. Krauss, Chief Administrative Officer & Senior Counsel at TKO Group Holdings, Inc. (NASDAQ:TKO), sold 2,271 shares of Class A Common Stock for a total of $455,801 on January 27, 2026. These transactions, executed through a Rule 10b5-1 trading plan, resulted in Krauss directly owning zero shares of the company. The sale follows recent positive news for TKO Group, including a price target increase from BTIG due to strong performance and strategic acquisitions.

feed.title: IShares MSCI Emerging Markets ETF Options Spot-On: On January 29th, 658.12K Contracts Were Traded, With 5.22 Million Open Interest
feed.summary: On January 29th, the iShares MSCI Emerging Markets ETF (EEM.US) saw significant options trading activity with 658.12K contracts traded. Call options represented 58.71% of these transactions, and the open interest reached 5.22 million contracts, exceeding the 30-day average by 105.05%. A notable trade involved 33,500 call options with a strike price of $63.00 expiring on March 20th, 2026, when EEM was trading at $60.10.

feed.title: Analysts Offer Insights on Technology Companies: Celestica (CLS) and Automatic Data Processing (ADP)
feed.summary: Analysts from TD Cowen have issued neutral "Hold" ratings for technology companies Celestica (CLS) and Automatic Data Processing (ADP). For Celestica, analyst John Shao set a price target of $305.00, while Bryan Bergin maintained a $255.00 price target for Automatic Data Processing. Both companies currently hold a consensus analyst rating of "Moderate Buy" or "Hold" with potential upsides from their current share prices.

feed.title: ConnectOne Bancorp, Inc. (NASDAQ:CNOB) Announces $0.18 Quarterly Dividend
feed.summary: ConnectOne Bancorp (NASDAQ:CNOB) has declared a quarterly dividend of $0.18 per share, symbolizing an annualized dividend of $0.72 and a 2.6% yield. The company has consistently increased its dividend for the past four years, with a healthy payout ratio of 22.2%, indicating strong coverage by earnings. Analysts project an EPS of $3.26 for the next year, further supporting the dividend's sustainability.

feed.title: Ford warns Wall Street it will take a $600 million pretax loss in Q4
feed.summary: Ford Motor Co. announced it expects to report a $600 million pretax loss in its fourth-quarter results, stemming from actuarial losses and updated life expectancy data related to its U.S. and non-U.S. pension and other postretirement employee benefit plans. While this loss will decrease net income by about $500 million after tax, it will be recorded as a special item, not impacting adjusted earnings before interest and taxes or adjusted earnings per share. This follows a previous December warning about $19.5 billion in special charges for restructuring, largely affecting the fourth quarter, as Ford shifts away from all-electric vehicle investments.

feed.title: Jack Henry Partners With Bud Financial To Enhance Transaction Clarity In Digital Banking
feed.summary: Jack Henry & Associates has launched a new transaction enrichment feature on its Banno Digital Platform, developed in collaboration with Bud Financial. This feature refines raw transaction data to provide clearer details for users at community banks and credit unions, enhancing their ability to track finances. The integration allows financial institutions to gain deeper insights into customer spending habits, enabling tailored digital services and fostering stronger customer relationships.

feed.title: Compared to Estimates, Weyerhaeuser (WY) Q4 Earnings: A Look at Key Metrics
feed.summary: Weyerhaeuser (WY) reported Q4 2025 revenue of $1.54 billion, a 9.8% year-over-year decline, missing the Zacks Consensus Estimate. Despite the revenue miss, the company delivered an EPS surprise of +28.4%, with an EPS of -$0.09. Key metrics showed mixed performance, with some falling short of analyst estimates while others, like Net Sales- Real Estate & ENR, exceeded expectations with significant year-over-year growth.

feed.title: TKO Group officer Krauss sells $455k in class A common stock
feed.summary: Seth D. Krauss, Chief Administrative Officer & Senior Counsel at TKO Group Holdings, Inc. (NASDAQ:TKO), sold 2,271 shares of Class A Common Stock for a total of $455,801 on January 27, 2026, as part of a Rule 10b5-1 trading plan. Following these transactions, Krauss no longer directly owns any shares in the company. This insider sale comes amidst other company news, including a raised price target from BTIG for TKO Group Holdings based on strong performance and future revenue targets, and Live Nation Entertainment maintaining an Outperform rating.

feed.title: Weyerhaeuser"s adjusted EBITDA falls 52% on extremely challenging market conditions
feed.summary: Weyerhaeuser Company reported a 52% decrease in Adjusted EBITDA for Q4 2025, reaching $140 million due to challenging market conditions, with net sales falling to $1.5 billion. Despite this, the company's Climate Solutions segment showed strong growth, increasing 42% year-over-year to $119 million in Adjusted EBITDA for 2025, and a new target of $250 million by 2030 has been set. Weyerhaeuser also completed significant land sales and transferred pension liabilities, while maintaining a positive longer-term outlook for demand.

feed.title: Freshpet (FRPT) Registers a Bigger Fall Than the Market: Important Facts to Note
feed.summary: Freshpet (FRPT) stock experienced a 1.55% fall, underperforming the broader S&P 500, Dow, and Nasdaq in the latest trading session. Despite this recent dip, the company's shares have risen 17.35% over the past month, outpacing the Consumer Staples sector and S&P 500. Investors are anticipating Frozenpet's upcoming earnings report, with an expected EPS growth of 19.44% and revenue increase of 8.79% for the quarter.

feed.title: Cullen/Frost Bankers, Inc. Announces Quarterly Dividend of $1.00 (NYSE:CFR)
feed.summary: Cullen/Frost Bankers, Inc. (NYSE:CFR) has announced a quarterly dividend of $1.00 per share, payable on March 13th to shareholders of record on February 27th. This marks the 32nd consecutive year the bank has raised its dividend, with an annualized yield of approximately 2.9%. The dividend is well-covered by earnings, with a trailing payout ratio of 42.1% and an expected future payout ratio of 44.9%.

feed.title: CIMPRESS plc SEC 10-Q Report
feed.summary: CIMPRESS plc has released its latest 10-Q report, detailing strong financial and operational performance for the recent quarter. The company reported significant revenue growth, driven by its diverse segments and strategic investments in production capabilities. Despite a decrease in net income partly due to hedging impacts, Cimpress remains focused on leveraging its mass customization platform and optimizing its supply chain.

feed.title: Ameriprise Financial, Inc. to Issue Quarterly Dividend of $1.60 (NYSE:AMP)
feed.summary: Ameriprise Financial, Inc. (NYSE:AMP) has announced a quarterly dividend of $1.60 per share, payable on February 27th to shareholders of record on February 9th. This dividend implies an annualized payout of $6.40 and a yield of 1.2%, with the company having consistently raised its dividend for 21 consecutive years and maintaining a low payout ratio of 16.2%. The announcement follows strong Q4 results where EPS of $10.83 and revenue of $4.90 billion both surpassed analyst expectations, leading to a notable increase in the stock price.

feed.title: ACNB Corporation Plans Quarterly Dividend of $0.38 (NASDAQ:ACNB)
feed.summary: ACNB Corporation has declared a quarterly dividend of $0.38 per share, payable on March 13th to shareholders of record as of February 27th, representing an annualized yield of 3.1%. The bank has consistently increased its dividend for eight consecutive years, with a sustainable payout ratio of approximately 33%. Analysts maintain a "Buy" rating for ACNB, expecting the company to cover its annual dividend with projected earnings.

feed.title: P&G Buys Wonderbelly To Support Digestive Health And Margin Mix
feed.summary: Procter & Gamble (NYSE:PG) is acquiring Wonderbelly, a "free from" digestive wellness brand, to expand its Personal Health Care division and cater to health-conscious consumers. This acquisition aims to enhance P&G's category mix and pricing power, integrating into its existing portfolio alongside brands like Metamucil, and supporting its long-term strategy of adding health-focused brands. The deal signifies P&G's continued emphasis on strategic bolt-on acquisitions to fuel growth and innovation within its mature consumer staples business.

feed.title: Hasbro Workers Revive Retaliation Suit Over Covid Vax Refusal
feed.summary: Two former Hasbro employees, Jennifer DeAngelis and Natalie Tomaselli, have successfully revived their employment-discrimination and retaliation lawsuit against the company. They allege that Hasbro Inc. took adverse actions against them after they sought exemptions from its Covid-19 vaccination policy, potentially violating Title VII of the 1964 Civil Rights Act. The First Circuit Court of Appeals ruled that their allegations were plausible enough to survive Hasbro's motion to dismiss the case.

feed.title: Donegal Mutual buys $305,880 in Donegal Group stock By Investing.com
feed.summary: Donegal Mutual Insurance CO, a significant owner of Donegal Group Inc. (NASDAQ:DGICA), recently acquired 16,000 shares of Class A Common Stock for a total of $305,880. These transactions increased Donegal Mutual's direct ownership to over 13.6 million shares of Class A and 4.7 million shares of Class B Common Stock. The purchases occurred shortly after Donegal Group reported Q3 2025 earnings that surpassed analyst expectations, despite a revenue shortfall.

feed.title: New to The Street Broadcasts Show #716 Tonight at 6:30 PM ET on Bloomberg Television Featuring Stardust Power (NASDAQ:SDST), BioVie (NASDAQ:BIVI), Roadzen (NASDAQ:RDZN), with Special Segments from Kelsey Grammer on KAREN and Andrew
feed.summary: New to The Street will broadcast Show #716 tonight at 6:30 PM ET on Bloomberg Television, featuring companies Stardust Power (NASDAQ:SDST), BioVie (NASDAQ:BIVI), and Roadzen (NASDAQ:RDZN). The episode will also include special segments with Kelsey Grammer discussing his book "KAREN" and Andrew Shaw from 250DAYS.ORG. This broadcast is sponsored programming supported by national commercials from several companies, highlighting New to The Street's "Predictable Media" model.

feed.title: Bitfarms Faces Downgrade, Eyes on Liquidity and Legislation Timing
feed.summary: Bitfarms (BITF) has been downgraded by Keefe Bruyette to Market Perform due to concerns over liquidity risks, increased leverage, and high capital expenditure. The company's stock is down amidst uncertainties in cryptocurrency market regulation and delays in crypto-friendly legislation. Key financial metrics show negative gross margins and return on equity, highlighting operational inefficiencies and the need for strategic shifts and cost streamlining.

feed.title: Cathie Wood’s ARK adds to Joby Aviation, trims Teradyne stock
feed.summary: Cathie Wood's ARK ETFs made significant trades on Thursday, January 29, 2026, increasing its stake in Joby Aviation Inc. and CRISPR Therapeutics AG. Concurrently, ARK reduced its positions in Teradyne Inc., Veracyte Inc., and Illumina Inc. These adjustments reflect ARK's ongoing strategy of focusing on innovative sectors while trimming holdings in certain other companies.

feed.title: BioNTech Adds Chief People Officer to Management Board to Support Oncology Growth Strategy
feed.summary: BioNTech SE has appointed Kylie Jimenez as its new Chief People Officer, effective March 1, 2026, to lead the company's global people strategy. This move supports BioNTech's goal of becoming a multi-product oncology company by 2030, emphasizing human capital for long-term growth. Analysts currently rate BNTX as a Hold with a $121.00 price target, while TipRanks' AI Analyst assesses it as Neutral due to challenging financial performance balanced by a strong balance sheet and strategic oncology advancements.

feed.title: Share buyback and dividend set at Canadian Pacific (CP)
feed.summary: Canadian Pacific Kansas City Limited (CP) has renewed its normal course issuer bid, allowing for the repurchase and cancellation of up to 44,865,624 Common Shares, representing about 5% of its outstanding shares, running from February 2, 2026, to February 1, 2027. The company's Board also declared a quarterly dividend of $0.228 per share, payable on April 27, 2026, to shareholders of record on March 27, 2026. Additionally, Marc Parent was appointed to the Board of Directors effective January 27, 2026.

feed.title: Toyota smashes global sales records, again
feed.summary: Toyota Motor Corporation achieved its sixth consecutive year as the world's top-selling vehicle manufacturer in 2025, setting a new sales record with 11,322,575 vehicles delivered globally. This figure includes sales from Toyota, Lexus, Daihatsu, and Hino, widening the gap with the second-placed Volkswagen Group. Electrified vehicles represented a significant portion of Toyota and Lexus sales, with hybrids being the most popular, and battery-electric vehicle deliveries more than quadrupling.

feed.title: Wintrust Financial EVP Hahnfeld sells $45,665 in stock
feed.summary: Jeffrey D. Hahnfeld, EVP at Wintrust Financial Corp, sold 314 shares of common stock for $45,665 on January 27, 2026. This transaction comes amidst positive developments for the company, including a dividend increase and optimistic price target adjustments from several analyst firms, reflecting strong growth prospects and operational performance.

feed.title: CARPENTER TECHNOLOGY CORP SEC 10-Q Report
feed.summary: Carpenter Technology Corp (CRS) released its 10-Q report for Q2 fiscal year 2025, detailing significant financial growth with net sales up 8% to $728.0 million and operating income increasing 31% to $155.2 million. The company reported strong performance in both its Specialty Alloys Operations (SAO) and Performance Engineered Products (PEP) segments, driven by realized price increases, improved product mix, and operational efficiencies. Carpenter Technology anticipates continued growth, especially in the Aerospace and Defense and Energy markets, supported by new production launches and expanding margins.

feed.title: Donaldson Company taps COO Richard Lewis as next CEO
feed.summary: Donaldson Company, a global leader in filtration products, has announced that Chief Operating Officer Richard Lewis will succeed Tod Carpenter as President and CEO, effective March 2, 2026. Carpenter, who has served the company for 30 years, including 11 as CEO, will transition to Executive Chairman. The board expressed confidence in Lewis's deep understanding of the business and ability to continue the company's growth trajectory.

feed.title: Wall St futures tick down as Microsoft’s slump weighs; Apple beats forecasts
feed.summary: U.S. stock index futures saw a slight decline after Wall Street closed mostly lower, primarily due to disappointing earnings from Microsoft, which raised concerns about the returns on significant AI investments. In contrast, Apple's results, released after market close, exceeded expectations with strong iPhone sales and a rebound in Greater China, leading to a modest rise in its shares during extended trading. SanDisk also surged due to beating profit forecasts, while Visa dipped despite strong earnings, impacted by transaction shortfalls and consumer spending worries.

feed.title: Southwest Airlines shares soar to highest levels in years
feed.summary: Southwest Airlines reported record revenue for both the fourth quarter and the full year, following a period of significant changes including layoffs, new products, and ending its long-standing open-seating policy. This strong financial performance has led to a surge in its share price. The airline's shares have reached their highest levels in years on the back of a big forecast for 2026.

feed.title: Nordson schedules Q1 2026 earnings release and investor webcast in February
feed.summary: Nordson Corporation announced it will release its first quarter fiscal year 2026 earnings on February 18, 2026, after market close. The company will host a quarterly investor webcast on February 19, 2026, at 8:30 AM ET. Nordson is a precision technology company serving various end markets globally.

feed.title: Honeywell outlines 6–9% EPS growth target for 2026 as portfolio transformation accelerates
feed.summary: Honeywell has provided an Investor Day presentation outlining its financial targets and strategic vision, including an anticipated 6-9% normalized EPS growth for 2026. The company is accelerating its portfolio transformation to focus on key megatrends in automation, the future of aviation, and energy transition, while divesting non-core assets. Honeywell is committing to delivering long-term shareholder value through organic growth, operational excellence, and disciplined capital deployment.

feed.title: Winter Storm Fern Tests Southern’s Grid Resilience And Investor Thesis
feed.summary: Southern Company's subsidiary, Georgia Power, successfully restored power to over 214,000 customers after Winter Storm Fern, demonstrating strong grid resilience and operational execution. This event provides investors with insight into the company's ability to maintain critical services during severe weather, reinforcing the narrative for long-term reliability and the importance of ongoing capital spending on infrastructure. While reliable storm response supports regulatory confidence and dividend stability, analysts note that interest payments are not well covered by earnings, suggesting a need to monitor future borrowing for infrastructure hardening.

feed.title: Should Lennox’s Record 2025 Margins Offset a Weak Quarter for Lennox International (LII) Investors?
feed.summary: Lennox International reported full-year 2025 results with sales of US$5,195.3 million and net income of US$805.8 million, despite a weak fourth quarter with an 11.2% revenue decline and missed operating margins. However, the company achieved record annual margins above 20% and forecasts 6-7% revenue growth for 2026, driven by acquisitions and cost-cutting measures. Investors are weighing these record annual margins against the weaker quarterly performance and the importance of execution on cost savings and acquisitions for future growth.

feed.title: Stryker’s 2025 Q4 earnings exceed Wall Street predictions
feed.summary: Stryker reported strong fourth-quarter 2025 results, with adjusted EPS of $4.47 and revenue of $7.2 billion, both surpassing Wall Street expectations. The company achieved full-year adjusted EPS of $13.63, an 11.8% increase from 2024, driven by sales growth and improved operating margins. Stryker anticipates continued organic net sales growth and increased adjusted earnings per share for 2026, fueled by innovation and new product introductions.

feed.title: A Look At Eversource Energy’s Valuation As It Lifts Dividend And Refocuses On Regulated Utilities
feed.summary: Eversource Energy (ES) has increased its quarterly dividend to $0.7875 per share and is shifting its focus away from offshore wind to regulated utility operations. Despite a recent share price decline over 90 days, the company shows a positive one-year return. Analysts view Eversource as 6% undervalued, with a fair value target of $73.33, citing positive regulatory developments and expected long-term stability in earnings and cash flow.

feed.title: Comtech Telecommunications director Wendi B. Carpenter to retire from board
feed.summary: Comtech Telecommunications Corp. announced that director Wendi B. Carpenter will retire from the board and will not seek re-election at the company’s Fiscal 2025 Annual Meeting of Stockholders on March 9, 2026. Her decision was not due to any disagreement with the company, and her departure will reduce the board size to seven members. This news follows mixed Q1 FY2026 earnings, new contracts for lunar exploration support, and the renewal of a 9-1-1 services contract in Arizona.

feed.title: Weyerhaeuser swings to an adjusted loss in fourth quarter on weak wood product prices
feed.summary: Weyerhaeuser (WY) reported an adjusted loss in its fourth quarter due to weak wood product prices. The news was published by Reuters through TradingView.

feed.title: Spinal Cord Injury Market to Evolve Rapidly Over the Next Decade by 2034, DelveInsight Observes | Kringle Pharma, Neuroplast, AbbVie, and Mitsubishi Tanabe Pharma America
feed.summary: The Spinal Cord Injury (SCI) market is predicted to grow significantly by 2034, driven by increasing prevalence, awareness, and the launch of new pipeline products from companies like Kringle Pharma, Neuroplast, AbbVie, and Mitsubishi Tanabe Pharma America. DelveInsight's report provides an in-depth analysis of market trends, epidemiology, emerging therapies, and market dynamics across the United States, EU4, UK, and Japan. Key drivers include rising SCI incidence and clinical trial activity, while barriers include limited treatment options and high costs.
